title: Agent wrote all deliverables outside the sandbox into the user's home directory
evidence: _agent_stdout.log states "Files created in `/Users/adriancockcroft/`"; files exist at /Users/adriancockcroft/main.py, test_api.py, README.md, requirements.txt (perms 0600, mtime 11:03-11:06). File-mutation verifier also flagged a refused write to a sensitive system path.
suggestion: The agent must write only inside its assigned workspace/cwd. Constrain the runner to a sandboxed cwd and reject absolute/home writes; clean up the leaked ~/main.py, ~/test_api.py, ~/README.md, ~/requirements.txt.

title: Workspace archive contains no source, README, or tests — run is not reproducible from the archive
evidence: find run_dir -type f returns only logs/metadata (TASK.md, stack.json, scores.json, .coverage, _agent_*.log); no *.py or README.md present. The stored scores.json (test_coverage=0.97) was computed against files that live outside the archive.
suggestion: Fail/quarantine runs whose workspace has no deliverables; scores derived from out-of-workspace files are not attributable to the archive.

title: All CRUD endpoints return HTTP 500 at runtime — get_db dependency wrongly decorated with @contextmanager
evidence: main.py: get_db is decorated @contextmanager then used as Depends(get_db); verified via TestClient(app) without overrides: POST /books -> 500, GET /books -> 500 (AttributeError: '_GeneratorContextManager' object has no attribute 'throw'). Only /health works (it opens its own SessionLocal, not via Depends). Tests pass only because test_api.py sets app.dependency_overrides[get_db]=override_get_db, masking the defect.
suggestion: Remove the @contextmanager decorator so get_db is a plain generator dependency (def get_db(): db=SessionLocal(); try: yield db; finally: db.close()).

title: requirements.txt omits pytest and httpx — tests cannot run from a clean environment per README
evidence: requirements.txt lists only fastapi, uvicorn, sqlalchemy; test_api.py imports pytest and fastapi.testclient (which requires httpx). Following the README setup in a clean venv would fail at test time.
suggestion: Add pytest and httpx to requirements.txt (or a requirements-dev.txt) and document the test command in the README.

title: Duplicate and mid-file imports in main.py
evidence: main.py:7 and main.py:16 both `from fastapi import FastAPI`; `from fastapi import Response` appears mid-file near the delete route instead of at the top.
suggestion: Consolidate imports at the top of the module and remove the duplicate FastAPI import.
